description: P(harm) for git write operations (commit, push, add) in medium-risk contexts. Live telemetry shows these are the dominant source of benign_review friction (32-63% of episodes). Git commits in a governed coding session are overwhelmingly benign.
P(harm) for read-only git operations (status, log, diff, show). Structurally safe — no writes to repository or system.
P(harm) for git branch operations (checkout, branch create/delete) in medium-risk contexts. Reversible; low harm probability in a governed coding session.

P(harm) for system execution in medium-risk contexts (running tests, linters, build scripts). Live telemetry shows these are a second major source of benign_review friction. 148 observed cycles show 0 safety violations from test runners and CI scripts in this governed development session.

P(harm) for system write operations to source or config files in medium-risk contexts. Live telemetry: 1666 correct_accepts and 0 safety violations from file writes in this session. Medium-tier writes to code files are routinely benign under REMORA governance.

P(harm) for outbound network read operations (web fetch, API GET, documentation lookup) in medium-risk contexts. External calls carry inherent risk (SSRF, data leakage) but are routinely benign in a governed development workflow. Higher than git/system due to external surface.

P(harm) for development tool execution (npm, pip, pytest, make) in medium-risk contexts. Package managers and test runners carry network/exec risk, but are routinely benign under REMORA governance. 148 cycles with 0 safety violations from dev tooling.
